---
title: "Client Report - The Flight Delay Dilemma"
subtitle: "Unit 3 Complete (Tasks 1-3 & Stretch)"
author: "David Martinez"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
execute: 
  warning: false
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd 
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)

```

## Executive Summary
This project analyzes airport flight delays to identify the worst offenders for weather-related issues and determine the optimal month for travel. The raw JSON data contained multiple inconsistencies (e.g., -999, "n/a", and blank strings) which were standardized into a tidy format. Through proportional analysis, I determined that smaller airports suffer higher percentages of weather delays, and the best month to fly to avoid delays overall is November. The stretch goal was completed, providing a side-by-side comparison of delay reasons across major hubs.

## Methods & Data Cleansing
First, we imported the raw JSON data and fixed all varied missing data types to consistently use np.Nan


```{python}
# Load Data
df = pd.read_json("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byuidatascience/data4missing/master/data-raw/flights_missing/flights_missing.json")

# Standardize Missing Values
delay_cols = [col for col in df.columns if col.startswith('num_of_delays_')]
df[delay_cols] = df[delay_cols].apply(pd.to_numeric, errors='coerce')
df['num_of_flights_total'] = pd.to_numeric(df['num_of_flights_total'], errors='coerce')
df['month'] = df['month'].replace({'n/a': np.nan, 'Febuary': 'February'})
df['airport_name'] = df['airport_name'].replace('', np.nan)
df = df.replace(-999.0, np.nan)

# Drop rows with completely missing airport names or months for the core analysis
df_clean = df.dropna(subset=['airport_name', 'month']).copy()

```

## Results & Analysis
**1. Which airport has the worst delays?**
To determine the "worst" airport, I calculated the proportion of flights delayed by weather relative to total flight volume, ensuring massive hubs weren't unfairly penalized simply for having more flights.

```{python}
weather_delays = df_clean.groupby('airport_name').agg(
    total_flights=('num_of_flights_total', 'sum'),
    weather_delayed_flights=('num_of_delays_weather', 'sum')
).reset_index()

weather_delays['proportion_weather_delayed'] = weather_delays['weather_delayed_flights'] / weather_delays['total_flights']
weather_delays = weather_delays.sort_values('proportion_weather_delayed', ascending=False)
weather_delays['airport_name'] = pd.Categorical(
    weather_delays['airport_name'],
    categories=weather_delays['airport_name'],
    ordered=True
)

weather_plot = (
    ggplot(weather_delays, aes(x='airport_name', y='proportion_weather_delayed')) +
    geom_bar(stat='identity', fill='#0084ff') +
    labs(x='Airport', y='Proportion of Flights Delayed by Weather', title='Proportion of Weather Delays by Airport') +
    theme(axis_text_x=element_text(angle=45, hjust=1))
)
weather_plot

```

**2. What is the best month to fly?**
By calculating the percentage of total delays across all airports by month, we can see that November has the lowest percentage of delayed flights, making it the safest bet for travel.

```{python}
month_grp = df_clean.groupby("month")[["num_of_delays_total", "num_of_flights_total"]].sum().reset_index()
month_grp["percentage"] = (month_grp["num_of_delays_total"] / month_grp["num_of_flights_total"] * 100).round(1)

# Sort chronologically
month_order = ["January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June", "July", "August", "September", "October", "November", "December"]
month_grp['month'] = pd.Categorical(month_grp['month'], categories=month_order, ordered=True)

month_plot = (
    ggplot(month_grp, aes(x='month', y='percentage')) +
    geom_bar(stat='identity', fill='#2ca02c') +
    labs(title="Percentage of Delayed Flights by Month", x="Month", y="% Delayed") +
    theme(axis_text_x=element_text(angle=45))
)
month_plot
```


**3. Comparing Delay Types (Stretch)**
Using pd.melt, I restructured the data to compare Weather, Carrier, and Security delays side-by-side. Weather and Carrier issues dominate the reasons for delays across all major airports.

```{python}
airport_grouped = df_clean.groupby('airport_code').agg(
    total_delays=('num_of_delays_total', 'sum'),
    weather_delays=('num_of_delays_weather', 'sum'),
    carrier_delays=('num_of_delays_carrier', 'sum'),
    security_delays=('num_of_delays_security', 'sum')
).reset_index()

airport_grouped['Weather'] = ((airport_grouped['weather_delays'] / airport_grouped['total_delays']) * 100)
airport_grouped['Carrier'] = ((airport_grouped['carrier_delays'] / airport_grouped['total_delays']) * 100)
airport_grouped['Security'] = ((airport_grouped['security_delays'] / airport_grouped['total_delays']) * 100)

airport_melt = airport_grouped.melt(
    id_vars='airport_code', 
    value_vars=['Weather', 'Carrier', 'Security'],
    var_name='Delay Reason',
    value_name='Percentage'
)

stretch_plot = (
    ggplot(airport_melt, aes(x='airport_code', y='Percentage', fill='Delay Reason')) + 
    geom_bar(stat='identity', position='dodge') +
    labs(title="Proportion of Delay Types by Airport", x="Airport Code", y="Percentage of Total Delays")
)
stretch_plot
```
